Install prompts as skills uninstall ... Remove installed skills installed List installed skills update Update installed skills Bundles bundles List available bundles bundle show Show bundle details Registry registry status Show cache status registry refresh Force cache refresh Utilities open Open prompt in browser doctor Check environment about About this tool completion Generate shell completions EXAMPLES jfp list --category ideation --json jfp search 'readme' --limit 5 jfp install idea-wizard readme-reviser jfp render my-prompt --VAR=value DOCUMENTATION https://jeffreysprompts.com/docs ``` **Reasoning:** Comprehensive help is essential for CLI discoverability. Agents and humans both benefit from well-organized documentation.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:34:02.703131871-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T02:41:23.609032273-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T02:41:23.609032273-05:00","close_reason":"Help command already fully implemented with: organized categories, examples, documentation links, pager support, and JSON output for agents.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-7g3","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-xdt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:37:43.460016979-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-7k5","title":"EPIC: Comprehensive Real Test Suite (No Mocks)","description":"Replace all mock-based unit tests with real tests using actual implementations. Web component tests use Vitest + Testing Library with real DOM rendering. All test suites green."} -{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-7l29","title":"Implement keyboard shortcuts reference modal","description":"## Origin Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-axfu. Keep both repos in sync. ## Task Create a discoverable keyboard shortcuts reference modal. ## Why This Matters - Helps users learn shortcuts - Power user feature discovery - Accessibility improvement - Professional UX ## Features ### 1. Shortcuts Modal - Press ? to open - Categorized shortcuts - Search shortcuts - Visual key display ### 2. Shortcut Categories | Category | Examples | |----------|----------| | Navigation | G+D (Dashboard), G+L (Library) | | Actions | N (New prompt), S (Save) | | Search | / (Focus search), Esc (Clear) | | Editor | Cmd+B (Bold), Cmd+I (Italic) | | Selection | Cmd+A (Select all), Shift+Click | ### 3. Key Display - Platform-aware (Cmd vs Ctrl) - Visual key caps - Combination indicators (+) - Sequence indicators (then) ### 4. Customization (Premium) - Customize shortcuts - Conflict detection - Reset to defaults - Import/export ## Implementation ### Shortcuts Registry ```typescript const shortcuts: ShortcutConfig[] = [ { id: 'new-prompt', keys: ['n'], description: 'Create new prompt', category: 'actions', action: () => router.push('/prompts/new'), when: 'not-editing', }, { id: 'save', keys: ['mod+s'], description: 'Save current item', category: 'actions', action: () => save(), when: 'editing', }, { id: 'search', keys: ['/'], description: 'Focus search', category: 'search', action: () => searchRef.current?.focus(), when: 'not-editing', }, ]; ``` ### Shortcuts Provider ```typescript function ShortcutsProvider({ children }) { useEffect(() => { const handler = (e: KeyboardEvent) => { // Check for ? to open modal if (e.key === '?' && !isEditing()) { setShowModal(true); return; } // Find matching shortcut const shortcut = findMatchingShortcut(e, shortcuts); if (shortcut && canExecute(shortcut)) { e.preventDefault(); shortcut.action(); } }; window.addEventListener('keydown', handler); return () => window.removeEventListener('keydown', handler); }, []); return ( {children} ); } ``` ### Modal Component ```tsx function ShortcutsModal() { const { shortcuts, showModal, setShowModal } = useShortcuts(); const [search, setSearch] = useState(''); const grouped = groupBy(shortcuts, 'category'); const filtered = filterBySearch(grouped, search); return ( Keyboard Shortcuts setSearch(e.target.value)} /> {Object.entries(filtered).map(([category, items]) => (

{category}

{items.map(shortcut => ( ))}
))} ); } ``` ## Files to Create - src/lib/shortcuts/registry.ts - src/contexts/shortcuts-context.tsx - src/components/shortcuts/shortcuts-modal.tsx - src/components/shortcuts/shortcut-key.tsx - src/hooks/use-shortcuts.ts ## Success Criteria - ? opens modal - All shortcuts documented - Platform-aware display - Searchable list ","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:19:52.246227559-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T01:19:52.246227559-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-7l29","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3q8a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:25:32.068038253-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} +{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-7l29","title":"Implement keyboard shortcuts reference modal","description":"## Origin Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-axfu. Press ? to open.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-7l29","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3q8a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:25:32.068038253-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-7m9s","title":"Integration Tests: Error Monitoring Hooks","description":"## Origin Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-kl6p. Keep both repos in sync. ## Task Create integration tests for error monitoring hooks (Sentry configuration). ## Why This Matters - Ensures errors are captured consistently - Prevents silent failures in production ## Test Scenarios 1. Simulated server error sends Sentry event (stubbed DSN) 2. Client error boundary captures and reports 3. RequestId included in Sentry context ## Logging Requirements - Log capture calls with requestId - Ensure no PII in payloads ## Files to Create - tests/integration/monitoring/sentry-server.test.ts - tests/integration/monitoring/sentry-client.test.ts ## Success Criteria - Monitoring hooks wired correctly - Events carry correlation IDs - Tests run without external network calls ","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:29:39.018028106-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T01:29:39.018028106-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-7m9s","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-v6re","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:30:01.852350996-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-7mo","title":"Create transcript processing scripts -- Details: scripts/extract-transcript.ts uses cass to export session JSONL. scripts/process-transcript.ts converts to ProcessedTranscript JSON. scripts/redact-transcript.ts removes secrets/paths. scripts/validate-publication.ts fails CI on leaks.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:27:14.329042173-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T16:55:29.22539911-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T16:55:29.22539911-05:00","close_reason":"All 4 transcript scripts complete: extract-transcript.ts, process-transcript.ts, redact-transcript.ts, validate-publication.ts","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-7mo","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-rq8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:39:16.031429198-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-7nb","title":"Mobile testing and polish -- Details: Test iPhone SE through iPad Pro. 44px minimum touch targets. Removed all mock.module() usage.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-7z4f","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-5mm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:55:04.100415664-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-7zeg","title":"Feature: Pre-Release Test Gates","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"agent-claude","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:43:43.761993855-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T19:36:28.272724455-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T19:36:28.272724455-05:00","close_reason":"Enhanced pre-release test gates in release.yml. Added lint, validation, typecheck, unit test, build, and E2E gates."} -{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-80au","title":"Implement command palette (Cmd+K)","description":"## Origin Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-87ys. Keep both repos in sync. ## Task Implement a command palette for quick navigation and actions. ## Features ### Trigger - Cmd+K (Mac) / Ctrl+K (Windows) - Visible in header (optional button) - Focus immediately on open ### Search - Search prompts, collections, actions - Fuzzy matching - Recent items first - Category grouping ### Actions Available - Navigate to pages - Search prompts - Create new prompt - Open settings - Toggle dark mode - View keyboard shortcuts - Open documentation - Switch workspace (if applicable) ### Navigation Items - Home, Library, Search - Collections (list user's collections) - Settings, Billing, Profile - Admin (if admin user) ### Quick Actions - "New prompt" → Opens editor - "New collection" → Opens modal - "Copy last prompt" → Copies - "Export library" → Triggers export ### UI/UX - Modal overlay with backdrop - Search input at top - Grouped results below - Arrow key navigation - Enter to select - Escape to close - Recent searches shown ## Implementation - Use cmdk library (or similar) - Integrate with TanStack Query for data - Keyboard event handling - Focus trap in modal ## Files to Create - src/components/command-palette/index.tsx - src/components/command-palette/command-item.tsx - src/components/command-palette/command-group.tsx - src/hooks/use-command-palette.ts ## Success Criteria - Opens instantly - Search is fast - Actions work correctly - Keyboard navigation smooth - Accessible ","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:19:26.469409772-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T01:19:26.469409772-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-80au","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3q8a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:25:31.684540761-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} +{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-80au","title":"Implement command palette (Cmd+K)","description":"## Origin Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-87ys. Keep both repos in sync. ## Task Implement a command palette for quick navigation and actions. ## Features ### Trigger - Cmd+K (Mac) / Ctrl+K (Windows) - Visible in header (optional button) - Focus immediately on open ### Search - Search prompts, collections, actions - Fuzzy matching - Recent items first - Category grouping ### Actions Available - Navigate to pages - Search prompts - Create new prompt - Open settings - Toggle dark mode - View keyboard shortcuts - Open documentation - Switch workspace (if applicable) ### Navigation Items - Home, Library, Search - Collections (list user's collections) - Settings, Billing, Profile - Admin (if admin user) ### Quick Actions - "New prompt" → Opens editor - "New collection" → Opens modal - "Copy last prompt" → Copies - "Export library" → Triggers export ### UI/UX - Modal overlay with backdrop - Search input at top - Grouped results below - Arrow key navigation - Enter to select - Escape to close - Recent searches shown ## Implementation - Use cmdk library (or similar) - Integrate with TanStack Query for data - Keyboard event handling - Focus trap in modal ## Files to Create - src/components/command-palette/index.tsx - src/components/command-palette/command-item.tsx - src/components/command-palette/command-group.tsx - src/hooks/use-command-palette.ts ## Success Criteria - Opens instantly - Search is fast - Actions work correctly - Keyboard navigation smooth - Accessible ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:19:26.469409772-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T21:35:22.166582528-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T21:35:22.166582528-05:00","close_reason":"Command palette already implemented as SpotlightSearch.tsx with: Cmd+K trigger, fuzzy search, category filtering, keyboard navigation (arrows/enter/escape), semantic search mode, recent searches, copy to clipboard, accessibility (role=dialog, aria labels, focus trap). See apps/web/src/components/SpotlightSearch.tsx","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-80au","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3q8a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:25:31.684540761-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-81d","title":"Implement jfp completion command for shell completions","description":"Part 6.1 of PLAN. 'jfp completion --shell zsh|bash|fish' outputs shell completion script. Completions for: command names, prompt IDs, category/tag values, bundle IDs. Usage: source <(jfp completion --shell zsh). Improves CLI discoverability and usability for power users.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:46:33.448582063-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T17:09:44.989090273-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T17:09:44.989090273-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented jfp completion command with bash/zsh/fish support in packages/cli/src/commands/completion.ts. Commit c7f6497.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-81d","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-xdt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:49:26.819599989-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-82jx","title":"Implement user following and activity feed","description":"## Origin Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-7ly5. ## Task Allow users to follow creators and see activity feed. ## Why This Matters - Social engagement - Stay updated on favorite creators - Build community - Increase retention ## Features ### 1. Following System - Follow/unfollow users - Following count display - Followers count display - Following list page ### 2. Activity Feed - New prompts from followed users - New collections from followed - Updates to followed content - Filtered by type ### 3. Notifications - New follower notification - Optional email digest - Activity summary ### 4. Discovery - Suggested users to follow - Popular creators - Similar to your interests ## Database Schema ```typescript export const userFollows = pgTable('user_follows', { id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(), followerId: uuid('follower_id').references(() => userProfiles.id).notNull(), followeeId: uuid('followee_id').references(() => userProfiles.id).notNull(), createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(), }, (table) => [ unique('uq_follow').on(table.followerId, table.followeeId), index('idx_follows_follower').on(table.followerId), index('idx_follows_followee').on(table.followeeId), ]); export const activityFeed = pgTable('activity_feed', { id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(), userId: uuid('user_id').references(() => userProfiles.id).notNull(), // Feed owner actorId: uuid('actor_id').references(() => userProfiles.id).notNull(), // Who did it activityType: text('activity_type').notNull(), // new_prompt, new_collection, etc. resourceType: text('resource_type'), resourceId: uuid('resource_id'), seen: boolean('seen').default(false), createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(), }, (table) => [ index('idx_feed_user').on(table.userId), index('idx_feed_time').on(table.createdAt), ]); ``` ## Implementation ### Follow Actions ```typescript async function followUser(followerId: string, followeeId: string) { await db.insert(userFollows).values({ followerId, followeeId }); await createNotification(followeeId, 'new_follower', { followerId }); await updateFollowCounts(followerId, followeeId); } ``` ### Feed Generation ```typescript async function getFeed(userId: string, limit = 20) { // Get activities from followed users return db.select() .from(activityFeed) .where(eq(activityFeed.userId, userId)) .orderBy(desc(activityFeed.createdAt)) .limit(limit); } ``` ### Fan-out on Write When user creates content: 1. Get all followers 2. Insert feed entry for each follower 3. Batch insert for efficiency ## UI Components - FollowButton - Follow/unfollow - FollowStats - Follower/following counts - ActivityFeed - Scrollable feed - SuggestedUsers - Users to follow ## Files to Create - src/lib/db/schema/follows.ts - src/lib/social/follows.ts - src/lib/social/feed.ts - src/app/api/users/[id]/follow/route.ts - src/app/(app)/feed/page.tsx - src/components/social/follow-button.tsx ## Success Criteria - Following works correctly - Feed shows relevant activity - Counts accurate - Performance acceptable ","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:19:00.68485604-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T01:19:00.68485604-05:00"} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-897","title":"Add error boundaries with fallback UI -- Details: Wrap search, prompt grid, detail modals with error boundaries. Show friendly error message + retry button. Log errors for debugging. Prevent full page crash from component failures.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:26:59.23087891-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T16:53:50.466750981-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T16:53:50.466750981-05:00","close_reason":"ErrorBoundary component created with fallback UI, retry button, and multiple variants. Providers wrapped with error boundaries for main content and SpotlightSearch."}
